# Single-factor linear model & the design matrix

Many marine studies compare **groups**: control vs treatment, inshore vs
offshore. Here we use an **ocean-acidification (OA)** experiment — fish reared in
control vs acidified seawater — and ask whether acidification changes our
response (say, growth).

## Coding a 2-level factor

A categorical predictor enters the model through a **design matrix**. With two
levels we use a single **0/1 indicator**:

| Treatment | indicator `trt` |
|-----------|-----------------|
| Control   | 0 |
| Acidified | 1 |

$$
y_i = \alpha + \beta\,\text{trt}_i
$$

- $\alpha$ — the **control** mean (when `trt = 0`).
- $\beta$ — the **difference** for acidified fish (the treatment effect).

If $\beta$'s 95% CI excludes 0, acidification credibly shifts the response.

## In FANGS

FANGS auto-encodes a text column like `treatment` (`"control"`, `"acidified"`)
into integers for you. You can write the model directly against the indicator,
e.g. `mu[i] <- alpha + beta * trt[i]`.

```
model {
  for (i in 1:N) {
    y[i] ~ dnorm(mu[i], sigma)
    mu[i] <- alpha + beta_trt * trt[i]
  }
  alpha ~ dnorm(0, 50)
  beta_trt ~ dnorm(0, 10)
  sigma ~ dunif(0, 50)
}
```

## Your task

Fit the single-factor model to `oa-study.csv` and check your posterior means for
the control mean $\alpha$ and the acidification effect.
